title: Fremya ciliata
intro: Fremya ciliata, also known as Fremont's fleabane or Fremont's goldenweed, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Fremya ciliata include sesquiterpene lactones, such as ciliatin and desacetylciliatin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Fremya ciliata is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used as a dried herb in capsules or as a topical application for skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Fremya ciliata is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as coffee or madder family. Fremya ciliata is further classified within the genus Fremya, a genus of flowering plants in the family Rubiaceae.
